raw_event.description: 84% tariff on Chinese integrated circuits and advanced semiconductors under Section 301

enriched_event.description: 84% tariff on Chinese integrated circuits and advanced semiconductors under Section 301
key_facts: HTS codes 8541 and 8542 are subject to the tariff
Korea and Taiwan are exempt from the tariffs
CHIPS Act-funded fabs are exempt from the tariffs with >50% US value content

pros: Elimination of 84% tariffs on Chinese imports
Qualification for 25% tax credit under CHIPS Act
cons: Potential labor premium of 2.3x China due to US labor costs
scenarios.summary: Reshoring all affected SKUs to US-based suppliers could result in annual cost savings of $14.5 million due to eliminated tariffs and potential tax credits, despite potential labor premium costs. This strategy mitigates supply chain risk and ensures compliance with the CHIPS Act. Lead times for reshoring range from 8-20 weeks, averaging 6 months.

pros: Lower labor costs compared to US
scenarios.summary: Nearshoring to Mexico can reduce tariff risk and lower labor costs, with a potential annual cost delta of -$2,339,000 and a lead time of 8 months. The top SKU actions include diversifying suppliers for IC-8542-001, MEM-8542-006, IC-8541-002, and IC-8542-004 to Foxconn Mexico, SK Hynix Mexico, STMicroelectronics Mexico, and Broadcom Mexico, respectively.
